name: boundary-reference-compliance
task: Do a full code review of electron/domains/settings/router.ts — check everything: security, types, error handling, architecture, and testing.
assertions: Response follows the SKILL.md review order: Security is checked FIRST before other categories
Response checks whether the settings router stores or exposes API keys and flags any secrets in plain text
Response checks whether database/store access goes through a centralized wrapper (not direct file system or store calls scattered in the router)
Response checks whether corresponding tests exist for the settings router and notes their absence if missing
Response uses the structured output format from SKILL.md: each issue has Severity, Location, Issue, and Fix fields (or equivalent structured format)
